figures.caption: Figure 2.3: The points 𝑅𝑧𝑖(𝑥) + 𝑧𝑖 are all mapped back to 𝑥 under the function 𝑓 from Lemma 2.1. Consequently, the reflected process𝑋𝑡 moves from 𝑦 to 𝑥 if and only if the Brownian motion 𝐵𝑡 moves from 𝑦 to 𝑥 or to any of the points 𝑅𝑧𝑖(𝑥) + 𝑧𝑖.
Figure 2.2: Simulation of a reflected Brownian motion (green) along with the non-reflected version (blue) that is used for its construction using Lemma 2.1.
Figure 3.1: Example of a domain 𝑀 ⊂ ℝ3 and its lower-dimensional representation 𝑀∗ ⊂ ℝ2 .
